helpful: Provisioned petition/BIO/QP text — the BIO's vehicle arguments and companion-case citations (Hadley 25-1158, Pena 25-1163) drove most of the analysis
statpack.json per-Term paid-vs-IFP grant rates (the .md tables blend fee classes)
CourtListener dockets endpoint for companion-petition pending status
gaps: fedcourts query returned zero rows for citation (Tyler 598 U.S. 631, Bennis 516 U.S. 442) and topic (takings) filters — a topic/citation index over the modern cert slice would make priors retrieval actually usable
No call-for-response (CFR) cut in the statpack; CFR after waiver is the strongest docket signal on paid petitions and has no committed base rate
The statpack lacks the salience-band table the prompt describes, so there was no band-level anchor for a CFR'd paid petition
CourtListener docket-entries endpoint returns nothing for scraped SCOTUS dockets, so companion dockets' distribution/relist history was unavailable
notes: RECAP search does not index SCOTUS dockets; the dockets REST endpoint with court=scotus + docket_number works. Corpus ranged reads were expensive (~110 MB per citation query) for zero rows.
